This terrorist attack constitutes a clear violation of UN Security Council Resolution 2817 and a dangerous escalation. The UAE reserves its full right to respond to such hostile actions.

summary: Pakistan's key mediator role intensifies. Army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir arrives in Tehran April 15 carrying Washington message for Round 2 talks. Previously mediated two-week ceasefire (April 7-8). Hosted Islamabad Round 1 talks (April 11-12, ended without deal). Trump praises Munir: 'doing a great job.' Pushing for talks before April 21 ceasefire expiry.

actions.text: Pakistan offers to host US-Iran direct talks in Islamabad; delivers US 15-point framework to Iranian officials; PM Sharif holds calls with Trump and Pezeshkian.
Pakistan confirms delivery of Iranian 5-point counterproposal to US; German FM Wadephul says US-Iran direct meeting in Pakistan being "prepared for very soon."

Pakistan Deputy PM Dar announces Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships to transit Hormuz (2/day). Saudi, Turkey, Egypt FMs arrive Islamabad for emergency peace summit. Pezeshkian calls PM Sharif: "trust is needed" for talks to succeed.

Islamabad summit Day 1: Saudi FM Prince Faisal, Turkish FM Fidan, Egyptian FM Abdelatty physically arrive in Islamabad. FM Ishaq Dar confirms Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships/day through Hormuz. Germany FM Wadephul says direct US-Iran meeting in Islamabad "very soon." China Wang Yi praises Pakistan mediation.

First two Pakistani-flagged ships (MV Multan and P-Akili crude tanker carrying 80M+ litres) confirmed transiting Hormuz under Iran-Pakistan deal. Ships en route to Karachi, expected to dock March 31. Islamabad hosts quadrilateral summit with Saudi, Turkish, and Egyptian FMs — day 1 of 2-day emergency peace negotiations.

Rubio-Araghchi direct talks reportedly imminent following Islamabad quadrilateral summit. Pakistan FM Dar confirmed the 20 Pakistani-flagged ships/day deal through Hormuz is operational; 2 ships/day transiting with IRGC escort. PM Shehbaz Sharif thanked regional allies for supporting Pakistan's mediation role. Iran conveyed formal response to US 15-point proposal through Pakistan. Potential direct Rubio-Araghchi meeting in Islamabad expected 'within days.' Trump reposted Dar's Hormuz announcement on Truth Social.
Co-authored 5-point peace plan with China. Serving as primary US-Iran mediator. Pakistan FM confirmed message relay between Washington and Tehran. Witkoff communicating through Pakistani channels.

Pakistan-led mediation (with Turkey/Egypt/Saudi) hitting structural incompatibility. Iran rejected US 15-point plan; demands reparations + sovereignty over Hormuz. Pakistan says will continue despite 'obstacles.'

FM Dar and Saudi FM Prince Faisal discussed China-Pakistan five-point peace initiative (ceasefire, talks, infrastructure protection, Hormuz reopening, peace framework). Uzbekistan, Egypt, Turkey back the plan. Iran FM Araghchi says 'deeply grateful to Pakistan' for mediation.

Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey submit 45-day ceasefire proposal (Islamabad Accord) to US and Iran. FM Asim Munir in overnight contact with VP Vance and FM Araghchi. Phase 1: ceasefire + Hormuz; Phase 2: permanent deal.

PM Sharif successfully mediated 2-week bombing suspension. Trump agreed to suspend attacks conditional on Hormuz reopening. Pakistan back-channel remained active even after Iran severed direct US communication.

Iran SNSC formally accepts ceasefire. Islamabad peace talks confirmed for Friday April 10 — VP Vance to lead US delegation. FM Asim Munir in contact 'all night' with Vance and Witkoff. China gave Iran 'last-minute nudge' to accept. Pakistan's role as primary mediator solidified.

Army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir visited Tehran carrying US message. Met FM Araghchi, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, IRGC commander Abdollahi. Pakistani sources hint 'major breakthrough' on nuclear freeze, 440kg HEU, Hormuz control. Attended Antalya quad meeting April 17.

PM Sharif held 45-min call with Pezeshkian after Touska seizure. Pezeshkian condemned US 'bullying.' Sharif consulted Saudi, Qatar, Turkey leaders. FM Dar spoke with Araghchi about talks. Hosting Round 2 despite IRNA declining — US delegation (Vance/Witkoff/Kushner) arriving Monday.

statements.speaker: Ishaq Dar, Deputy Prime Minister
statements.text: Pakistan has secured an agreement from Iran to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Pakistan stands ready to host direct US-Iran talks in Islamabad.

statements.speaker: PM Shehbaz Sharif
statements.text: We had a productive 1-hour call with President Pezeshkian. Pakistan calls for an end to all attacks and hostilities. We are ready to host direct US-Iran talks as a neutral venue. Pakistan stands for peace.

statements.speaker: FM Ishaq Dar
statements.text: Pakistan is very honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides in coming days. All four nations — Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt — express full support for US-Iran direct talks in Islamabad.

statements.speaker: Pakistan Foreign Ministry
statements.text: Pakistan has submitted a two-phase ceasefire framework alongside Egypt and Turkey. Phase 1: immediate ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

statements.speaker: PM Shehbaz Sharif
statements.text: Asked both sides for a two-week ceasefire window to finalize talks. Pakistan mediation channel remained the only active diplomatic link after Iran cut direct US contacts.

statements.speaker: PM Shehbaz Sharif
statements.text: Both parties have displayed remarkable wisdom. We invite US and Iranian delegations to Islamabad Friday April 10 for a conclusive agreement to settle all disputes.

statements.speaker: PM Shehbaz Sharif
statements.text: Islamabad talks confirmed for Friday April 10. VP Vance to lead US delegation. Iran Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf to lead Iranian delegation. Field Marshal Asim Munir in contact 'all night' with Vance and Witkoff.

statements.speaker: Pakistan Foreign Ministry
statements.text: We hope that the two sides continue with a positive spirit. The success of this diplomatic process depends on the seriousness and good faith of the opposing side, refraining from excessive demands. Pakistan will continue mediation efforts.

statements.speaker: Field Marshal Asim Munir
statements.text: Pakistan army chief arrives in Tehran with Interior Minister Naqvi carrying a message from Washington. Pushing for Round 2 talks before April 21 ceasefire expiry. Trump praises Munin: 'doing a great job.'

statements.speaker: PM Shehbaz Sharif
statements.text: Reaffirmed Pakistan's commitment to 'honest and sincere efforts' for regional peace in 45-min call with Iranian President Pezeshkian. Sharif also consulted leaders of Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey.

summary: Oman maintains neutrality while mediating Hormuz transit passage. Deputy FM-level talks with Iran exchanging proposals for smooth passage (April 4-5). Historical role as neutral intermediary in Gulf disputes.

actions.text: Deputy FM-level talks with Iran on Hormuz transit passage. Both sides exchanged proposals for 'smooth passage.' 3 Omani ships transited outside Iran's approved corridor.

statements.speaker: Omani Foreign Ministry
statements.text: Oman discussed possible options for ensuring smooth passage through the Strait with Iran. Both sides exchanged proposals that will be studied. Oman reaffirms its neutrality.

summary: Australia refuses to join US naval blockade of Hormuz. PM Albanese says Australia 'not been asked' and does not expect to deploy ships. Maintaining diplomatic distance from unilateral US action while supporting freedom of navigation.

actions.text: PM Albanese declines to join US Hormuz blockade. Says Australia 'not been asked' and does not expect to deploy ships.

statements.speaker: PM Anthony Albanese
statements.text: Australia has not been asked to participate in the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. We do not expect to deploy Australian ships to enforce the blockade.

summary: South Korea rebuffed Trump's call to join Project Freedom, demanding a UN mandate. Cargo ship HMM Namu (Panama-flagged) confirmed hit by two drone strikes in Strait of Hormuz (May 10 announcement). Seoul previously investigating possible Iranian attack. 4th tanker rerouted via Red Sea. Special envoy Chung Byung-ha dispatched to Tehran for safe passage of 26 Korean vessels.

actions.text: Special envoy Chung Byung-ha met Iran FM Araghchi in Tehran. Requested cooperation for safe Hormuz transit for 26 Korean vessels with 173 crew.

South Korean-operated cargo ship HMM Namu hit by explosion/fire in engine room while anchored near UAE in Strait of Hormuz. 24 crew (6 Korean, 18 foreign) evacuated safely. Vessel towed to Dubai. Government investigating possible Iranian attack.

South Korea announced findings that two unidentified airborne objects struck the stern of HMM NAMU about one minute apart, causing explosion and fire. Confirmed as drone attack. Responsibility not yet determined.

statements.speaker: Special Envoy Chung Byung-ha
statements.text: We request Iran's cooperation in ensuring the safe passage of South Korean vessels and the safety of our 40 nationals in the region.

statements.speaker: South Korean Foreign Ministry
statements.text: An explosion and fire occurred on the HMM Namu. All crew have been safely evacuated. We are investigating whether this was an external attack.

statements.speaker: South Korean Government
statements.text: South Korea will not join Operation Project Freedom without a UN mandate. Any military participation requires multilateral authorization.
